headline: Ethiopia material downgrade following May 5 2026 TPLF reconstitution of pre-war Tigray regional council and election of Debretsion Gebremichael as Regional President in direct defiance of the Pretoria Agreement architecture. Two rival authorities now claim Tigray governance (Tadesse Worede interim vs Debretsion-led restored council). Pretoria Agreement de facto dissolved per peace scholar Kjetil Tronvoll: 'this may trigger the outbreak of new armed conflict.' Compound: 15M+ Ethiopians need emergency food aid; 3.3M internally displaced; armed skirmishes between TPLF forces and federal army since January 2026. Published 10.9 / Critical-band already proximate to floor. Half-step down on ACT (state response collapse), ACC (dual-authority destroys accountability framework), SYS (Pretoria architecture dismantlement), INT (federal-Tigray compact non-compliance). Composite 10.9 → 4.7; floor candidacy retained but not designated this cycle pending federal response and active-conflict resumption documentation. Recommend material downgrade with floor candidacy carried forward.
key_evidence: May 5 2026: TPLF reconstituted pre-war Tigray regional council (suspended for 3.5 years), elected Debretsion Gebremichael as Regional President; report on Pretoria Agreement implementation approved unanimously prior to vote.
May 5 2026: TPLF Central Committee rejected federal interim administration extension. Two rival authorities now claim Tigray governance (Tadesse Worede interim vs Debretsion-led restored council).
Armed skirmishes between TPLF forces and federal army / government-aligned fighters since January 2026 — pre-existing armed-conflict resumption signal.
Peace scholar Kjetil Tronvoll: 'If mitigating efforts and a process of de-escalation are not introduced quickly, this may trigger the outbreak of new armed conflict.'
15M+ Ethiopians need emergency food aid; 3.3M internally displaced (UN/OCHA baseline).
HRW World Report 2026: existing Ethiopia chapter documents pre-existing pattern of arbitrary detention, restrictions on humanitarian access, and civilian-harm in Amhara and Oromia conflict theaters.

evidence.source: Addis Standard — TPLF says Tigray Council 'elects Debretsion Gebremichael as president'
evidence.finding: May 5 2026 reconstitution of pre-war regional council; Debretsion election; Pretoria Agreement implementation report.

evidence.source: Ethiopia Observer — TPLF reconstitutes pre-war regional council, elects Debretsion President
evidence.finding: Two rival authorities claim Tigray governance simultaneously; interim president Tadesse Worede asserts continuing mandate.

evidence.source: WHBL / AP — Tigray party restores pre-war government in threat to northern Ethiopia peace
evidence.finding: Tronvoll quoted: armed conflict outbreak risk if de-escalation not introduced quickly.
Pre-existing Amhara/Oromia conflict, arbitrary detention, humanitarian access restrictions baseline.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 1.5 confirmed. Pre-existing critical-band placement reflects systematic suppression of harm-signal channels (humanitarian access restrictions in Tigray and Amhara). No further drop warranted in single cycle.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 1.5 confirmed. 15M+ emergency food aid need and 3.3M IDP baseline already reflected in published score.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 1.0 floor. AC2 proportionality and AC4 response collapse — federal government response to TPLF reconstitution is the primary live test of structural-action capacity; with two rival authorities claiming Tigray governance, the institutional response to large-scale civilian-harm risk has structurally degraded. Half-step down to floor.
scoring_rationale.BND: 1.5 confirmed. Pretoria Agreement boundary fundamentally compromised; not a further drop within single-cycle scope but boundary-setting capacity already at critical-band.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1.0 floor. AB2 transparency: dual-authority context structurally destroys accountability framework; no clear interlocutor for harm reporting in Tigray theater. Half-step down.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 1.0 floor. S1 causal reasoning at federal-policy level cannot reach Tigray context with rival authority; Pretoria Agreement architecture (the systemic mechanism for post-war peace) is dismantled. Half-step down.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1.0 floor. I1 consistency: federal-Tigray compact non-compliance from both sides; declared peace commitments contradicted by rival-authority context. Half-step down.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstruction yields 4.7. Material change -6.2 points. Critical-band sustained (no band crossing — already in critical band). Floor candidacy retained but floor designation NOT proposed this cycle pending federal response and active-conflict resumption documentation.

negativeSignals: TPLF reconstitution of pre-war regional council (May 5 2026); dual rival authorities in Tigray; Pretoria Agreement de facto dissolved; armed-skirmish baseline since January 2026; 15M+ emergency food aid need; 3.3M IDP; pre-existing Amhara/Oromia conflict; humanitarian access restrictions.
positiveSignals: Pretoria Agreement framework remains formally on the books (not denounced); HRW chapter notes some legal-procedural civic-space functionality; federal government has not (yet) deployed military response to TPLF council reconstitution.
sector_context: Africa cluster — floor-proximate cluster includes Sudan (floor), South Sudan (floor), Eritrea (floor-proximate). Ethiopia distinguished by Pretoria Agreement architecture providing residual constitutional check now under structural threat. Floor candidacy carried forward.
open_actions_resolved: May 5-6 2026 TPLF reconstitution event absorbed into scoring
Pretoria Agreement architecture dismantlement registered as material change
Floor candidacy carried forward (not designated this cycle)
open_actions_carried: Watch: federal government response to TPLF council reconstitution (military, political, humanitarian)
Watch: any large-scale civilian-harm event in Tigray theater (catastrophic-trigger event)
Watch: humanitarian access status in Tigray, Amhara, Oromia
Floor designation re-evaluation if armed conflict resumes at scale
